questions.question: The Flynn effect refers to:

options.text: The observation that identical twins have nearly identical IQ scores
The steady rise in average IQ scores across generations over time
The tendency for IQ scores to decrease with age
The difference in IQ scores between males and females

questions.explanation: The Flynn effect is the well-documented observation that average IQ scores have been rising steadily (approximately 3 points per decade) across many countries. This rise is attributed to environmental factors such as improved nutrition, greater access to education, and increased environmental complexity.

questions.question: A test that produces consistent, stable scores when the same person takes it on multiple occasions demonstrates high:
questions.explanation: Reliability refers to the consistency of a test's results. Test-retest reliability, specifically, means that the test produces similar scores when the same person takes it on different occasions. A test can be reliable without being valid -- it may consistently measure something that is not actually what it claims to measure.
